videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: This talk addresses a critical failure mode in web-accessing agents: they may claim to have searched the web while actually falling back to stale training data due to blocking, CAPTCHAs, or fake pages. The speaker demonstrates the difference between blocked and unblocked agent runs and explains the technical barriers including anti-bot systems, JavaScript rendering, and CAPTCHA handling.

videos.title: How to Build a Business Using AI: From Idea to Launch in One Day
videos.summary: A YouTube video demonstrating Rocket 1.0, a platform designed to solve context loss between AI tools in workflows. The creator tests Rocket's ability to support rapid startup prototyping from idea to MVP by integrating research, notes, code generation, and team collaboration.

videos.title: AI agents can manage your passwords. Should we let them? Plus: The biggest Patch Tuesday ever.
videos.summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast discusses whether AI agents should be trusted to detect compromised passwords and change them automatically, examines Microsoft's June 2026 Patch Tuesday covering over 200 CVEs as a potential watershed moment in vulnerability management, and explores the trend of C-suite executives accepting greater cyber risk in pursuit of innovation.

videos.title: Minimax Code + M3 : Crazy Alternative to Claude,Codex!
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is an open-weights AI model designed for coding agents, tool use, and long-context tasks. It supports up to one million token context windows and is compatible with both Anthropic and OpenAI APIs. MiniMax Code provides an IDE and multi-agent collaboration features for development workflows.

videos.title: POV: A new feature enters the chat. 🕺✨
videos.summary: A Google for Developers YouTube video offering a relatable perspective on how software engineers react to and manage new feature releases. The video uses humor to resonate with developer experiences.

videos.title: New #1 open-source AI model is here!
videos.summary: A YouTube review of GLM 5.2, an open-source AI model claimed as the new best in its class. The video demonstrates capabilities including digital earth visualization, animation with voiceover, 3D model generation, ray tracing, music composition, and mathematical animation, with integration into Claude Code also covered.

videos.title: Fable 5 Replacement Just Dropped: Fusion (Fable Level AI)
videos.summary: OpenRouter introduced Fusion, a system that sends one prompt to multiple AI models, compares their responses, identifies blind spots, and synthesizes a stronger combined response. A budget Fusion configuration achieved results comparable to Fable 5 on deep research benchmarks at roughly half the cost.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with access to up-to-date AWS documentation, best practices, and tested procedures. It improves agent success rates and reduces wasted effort by providing structured knowledge instead of relying on outdated general knowledge.

videos.title: The Briefing: Financial Services
videos.summary: Anthropic hosted a virtual event called The Briefing: Financial Services where leaders from major banks discussed their deployments of Claude across their organizations. The event showcased practical applications of Claude in financial services.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Claude Fable 5 capabilities with a focus on prompt engineering and agentic loops for SaaS applications. The video covers implications of relying on closed-source models versus open-source alternatives and risks in the evolving AI SaaS landscape.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers shows how to run Claude Code through Amazon Bedrock for developers with data residency, compliance, or billing constraints. The walkthrough covers setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and guardrails for content governance.

videos.title: Building long-running AI agents with ADK
videos.summary: Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit (ADK) is an open source framework for building stateless, event-driven long-running AI agents. It demonstrates a multi-agent pattern where a coordinator agent delegates specialized tasks to sub-agents while maintaining clean reasoning chains.

videos.title: Better Free AI Image than Midjourney?
videos.summary: Jack Roberts argues that Ideogram 4.0 has solved the text-in-image problem that plagued prior models, claiming it outperforms Midjourney and Google's Nano Banana Pro on certain benchmarks while remaining free. He positions it as a viable alternative for designers generating brand assets.

videos.title: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer on Machiavelli's life and political thought. Palmer argues Machiavelli is widely misunderstood, explaining how his diplomatic experience in Renaissance Florence informed his insights on power and human nature, which he documented in his writings.

videos.title: Coding with ADK to beat a text adventure game
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech hosted a livestream on coding with the Agent Development Kit to solve a text adventure game. The session featured speakers Tilde Thurium and Abdel Sghiouar demonstrating practical application of ADK.

videos.title: Building with ADK & A2A: How multi-agent collaboration designs a game
videos.summary: Google Cloud presented a hands-on demo of Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Agent2Agent protocol (A2A) for multi-agent collaboration. The demo showed distinct AI agents working together on game architecture, asset rendering, and project management with human-in-the-loop oversight on Cloud Run.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that the focus on humanoid robots from companies like 1X and Unitree may be limiting, and that designing robots for human interaction and delight rather than pure utility will be essential as billions of robots enter society. She suggests that the real challenge in robotics is not achieving intelligence but addressing broader questions about design purpose and human integration.

videos.title: Is Unstructured Data The Key To Successful AI Deployments?
videos.summary: A discussion from Hyland CommunityLIVE 2026 argues that unstructured data is critical for enterprise AI success and agentic systems. The speakers, including Hyland's CEO and Erie Insurance's CIO, present case studies of how unstructured data combined with proper context helps AI agents drive business impact across healthcare, banking, and insurance.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: NVIDIA's approach to accelerating video diffusion inference combines quantization, latent caching, and step distillation to reduce denoising steps from fifty to as few as one or four. The techniques are composable, with distribution-based distillation training a student model to match teacher outputs rather than exact trajectories. FastGen packages the post-training and GPU sharding infrastructure to apply these optimizations at scale.

videos.title: Gemini Omni Tutorial: How to Use Gemini Omni AI for Beginners
videos.summary: A tutorial reviewing Google's Gemini Omni Flash for AI video editing, covering physics simulation, character consistency, scene memory, and workflow integration. The presenter evaluates where Omni changes editing workflows versus where it still falls short compared to alternatives like Sora and Seedance 2.

videos.title: Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
videos.summary: Kagenti applies identity-based security and delegation chains to secure multi-agent AI systems. The approach aims to prevent data leaks and invisible breaches in production agentic systems using open source infrastructure and zero-trust architecture.

videos.title: This command SAVED my broken AI coding session
videos.summary: A YouTube video from JavaScript Mastery describes a command that resolved issues in an AI-assisted coding workflow. The specific command and its mechanics are detailed in the full video.

videos.title: Tencent Workbuddy: RIP Claude,Codex! This is ACTUALLY quite a bit CRAZY!
videos.summary: Tencent WorkBuddy is a desktop AI productivity agent that reads files, analyzes spreadsheets, generates reports and presentations, and connects to external tools via integrations. It features over 100 domain-expert agents, task automation through Slack/Telegram/Discord/WeChat, and a skill marketplace for extended capabilities.

videos.title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
videos.summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power developer board with a Coral NPU accelerator for running Gemma and other on-device AI models. The board ships with peripherals including a screen, camera, and microphones, enabling edge AI applications like live translation, natural language control, and multimodal inference entirely on the device.

videos.title: Google Just Revealed What Comes After AGI And It’s Shocking
videos.summary: A YouTube video discussing Google DeepMind's "From AGI to ASI" paper, which argues that AGI may mark the beginning rather than the end of transformative AI progress. The video suggests that once human-level AI is replicated and optimized, the jump to superintelligence (ASI) could be the more significant milestone, with implications for how intelligence scales as an industrial process.

videos.title: Stop testing your SAAS manually
videos.summary: KaneAI is an AI-powered QA testing tool that accepts natural language descriptions of test cases, automatically generates and runs tests, and reports results. It claims to reduce testing effort and costs significantly compared to manual QA processes.

videos.title: This MCP makes Hermes Agent 10x more powerful
videos.summary: Apify MCP Connectors enhance Hermes Agent performance by 10x according to the video. The connectors provide universal MCP integration capabilities for improved agent functionality. David Ondrej demonstrates practical use of these connectors for AI workflow automation.

videos.title: How to build reliable software with AI agents
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech discusses how to build reliable software with AI agents, arguing that human-in-the-loop verification and testing are essential to ensure coding agents reduce technical debt rather than merely accelerate output. The video emphasizes maintaining rigorous code review processes when using AI-accelerated development.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
videos.summary: A video discussing Claude Fable 5's removal from availability and advocating for local AI deployment using Ollama as a mitigation strategy. It demonstrates building a self-hosted NotebookLM alternative and setting up model routing with OpenRouter to reduce dependency on single cloud providers.

videos.title: SpaceX’s IPO Triumph, Anthropic’s Fable Fumble, OpenAI’s Price War
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss SpaceX's IPO success, Anthropic's Fable rollout challenges, and OpenAI's potential price reductions. The conversation covers whether AI models are commoditizing and competitive positioning among major AI firms.

videos.title: The magic behind GenLatte: Flutter, Firebase, and Gemini
videos.summary: GenLatte is an innovative demo application that generates personalized user interface elements and images for latte art using Flutter, Firebase, and Gemini. It combines agentic features and Agent-to-UI technology to enable real-time co-creation of dynamic, on-demand visual experiences tailored to individual customer preferences.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is an open-source meta-harness that allows users to orchestrate multiple AI coding models, such as Claude Code, Codex, and others, as interchangeable workers. It enables workflows where different models handle different stages of coding tasks, includes governance and human-in-the-loop capabilities, and maintains sessions across multiple devices.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: The double iframe pattern nests an iframe within another iframe to work around Content Security Policy restrictions and sandbox escape vulnerabilities. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then loads the application HTML via srcdoc into the inner frame, preventing storage collisions and unauthorized access to parent application data.

videos.title: In production, which file actually gets loaded?
videos.summary: Google for Developers discusses which package file gets loaded in production environments when the same application code uses identical package names but exhibits different behavior after deployment. The video explores package import resolution and module loading in production contexts.

videos.title: What Is AI Pair Programming? AI Coding Tools for Developers
videos.summary: AI pair programming is an approach where AI assistants work alongside developers to enhance coding productivity, debugging, and code review processes. The technique transforms traditional development workflows by providing AI-driven support throughout the software development lifecycle.

videos.title: FULLY FREE Unlimited API + OpenCode: MiniMax M3,Step 3.7 Flash,Nemotron 3 Ultra,GLM,Kimi!
videos.summary: OpenRouter has released Fusion API, which combines responses from multiple models using a judge model architecture. Benchmarks claim Fable-level performance at half the cost, but real-world testing shows inconsistent results across diverse tasks and suggests it may be slower and more expensive than using individual models.

videos.title: Why I never trust fully generated Code
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery author expresses distrust in fully generated code from AI systems. The piece represents a viewpoint on code generation reliability and best practices for developers.

videos.title: The Internet Is No Longer Human
videos.summary: The video argues that bots and AI agents now dominate internet traffic and activity, with Google AI Overviews reducing user clicks, AI agents making purchases autonomously, and machine requests outnumbering human activity. It frames this as a fundamental shift in how the web operates and who controls its use.

videos.title: STOP Using Claude Code Without This Fable 5 Agentic OS
videos.summary: Chase AI provides a tutorial on building a Claude Code-based agentic operating system, covering architecture, memory management, observability, and skill customization. The video walks through JARVIS as an example implementation and offers three-step setup guidance.

videos.title: You Don't Need React Portals For Modals Anymore (react-call)
videos.summary: react-call is a lightweight utility library that enables calling React components as asynchronous functions without React Portals. It provides architectural patterns for layout isolation, singleton management, and state flow using three core concepts: Root and Stack, Upsert, and Mutation Flow.

videos.title: Type This Into Claude, It'll Make You Build 10x Faster.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese presents six power phrases designed to optimize Claude Code usage for faster development cycles. The video emphasizes prompting strategies endorsed by Claude Code's creator and Andrej Karpathy, claiming they improve project initiation, scaling, and execution without requiring better models or plugins.

videos.title: Smart AI routing explained
videos.summary: IBM Technology discusses smart AI routing as a paradigm shift in AI labs away from simply scaling model size toward intelligent request routing. The approach prioritizes cost efficiency and trust in model selection.

videos.title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
videos.summary: OpenAI's Codex introduces a /goal command and "Ralph Loop" that autonomously plans, executes, and self-corrects code generation tasks without user intervention. Users describe the desired outcome in plain English and the system handles the full implementation and testing cycle.

videos.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
videos.summary: Context engineering is used to guide AI agents in software design by structuring information through design systems. MCP and AI agents work together to ensure consistent application generation while maintaining adherence to design rules and constraints.

videos.title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
videos.summary: This workflow combines Obsidian wiki with Claude and NotebookLM to build a self-improving knowledge base for AI agents like Hermes. It uses automatic ingestion from Granola and cron jobs to continuously update the knowledge base with meeting notes, emails, and research, implementing Karpathy's LLM wiki principles.

videos.title: OpenRouter Fusion: They OFFICIALLY CLAIM THAT THIS MODEL beats FABLE?
videos.summary: AICodeKing evaluates OpenRouter's Fusion API, which uses parallel model invocations with a judge model to select answers. While OpenRouter claims Fable-level performance at lower cost, practical testing reveals inconsistent results across simulators, SVG generation, math, and coding tasks. The reviewer concludes Fusion is overhyped and does not surpass Fable in real-world scenarios.

videos.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
videos.summary: AI Search reviews recent developments across generative AI including new models like GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, and MiniMax M3, as well as research in avatar generation, text-to-speech, and video synthesis. The episode covers emerging techniques in attention mechanisms, world modeling, and diffusion-based generation.

videos.title: 3,000+ Tokens Per Second? Let's Stress Test Xiaomi's New Model: MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed
videos.summary: Xiaomi and TileRT released MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model achieving 3,000+ tokens per second throughput on standard hardware. The architecture combines selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and TileRT persistent GPU kernel runtime to minimize inference bottlenecks.

videos.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
videos.summary: David Ondrej reports that Fable 5 has been banned and characterizes this as the beginning of a permanent underclass. The video appears to discuss concerns about AI access and platform governance.

videos.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
videos.summary: A YouTube video discusses the US government shutdown of Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models after three days of public availability, raising concerns about government control over frontier AI access and whether powerful AI systems will remain available to the general public.

videos.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
videos.summary: A YouTube video argues that GPT-6's delayed launch has allowed competitors including Microsoft, Apple, and Google to reduce dependence on OpenAI through alternative AI models and integrations. The video discusses recent competitive losses including Microsoft's Project Polaris replacing GPT in GitHub Copilot, Apple's $1B annual Gemini deal, and Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 leading benchmarks.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the sudden removal of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 following a US government export-control directive allegedly triggered by a jailbreak. He covers the regulatory action, reasons for the shutdown, and shares benchmarking results comparing Fable 5 to Opus 4.8.

videos.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
videos.summary: Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, a model that applies diffusion-based image generation techniques to text generation using uniform state diffusion. The architecture shifts local LLMs from memory-bound to compute-bound, achieving generation speeds exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs through multi-pass bidirectional refinement of token sequences.

videos.title: This AI Chinese model is 15X cheaper?
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a long-context AI model available on OpenRouter that uses sparse attention to process documents 15.6x faster than standard models while reducing compute costs to one-twentieth of the previous generation.

videos.title: GLM-5.2 (Fully Tested): I got EARLY ACCESS & This MODEL is CRAZY!
videos.summary: GLM-5.2 is an improved model from Z AI featuring a 1 million token context window, open-weight plans with potential MIT licensing, and strong coding performance at 81.43 on benchmarks, positioning it as a cost-effective alternative to Opus 4.8 and other premium models at around $8 per month through the GLM Coding Plan.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: BREAKING: US Government BANNED Mythos and Fable
videos.summary: The US government allegedly issued an export control directive requiring Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers immediately. Better Stack reports this as breaking news via a YouTube video with a link to Anthropic's news page.

videos.title: Google’s New AI Just Broke The AI Speed Limit: DiffusionGemma
videos.summary: Google released DiffusionGemma, an open AI model that generates text using a different approach and achieves up to four times faster inference on dedicated GPUs. The model supports a 256-token canvas and includes self-correction capabilities.

videos.title: Inspect and live-edit 3D WebGL elements
videos.summary: Google demonstrated the HTML-in-Canvas API, which enables inspection and live-editing of 3D WebGL elements while maintaining UI accessibility, searchability, and inspectability. The approach integrates HTML content with Canvas rendering to preserve developer tooling compatibility.

videos.title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
videos.summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that helps developers build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It offers multiple interfaces including a standalone 2.0 desktop app, IDE, CLI, and SDK for developers to choose their preferred development environment.

videos.title: AIventure: Vibe Coding Journey
videos.summary: AIventure is an open source retro dungeon crawler game built on Angular and Phaser.js that teaches developers about generative AI through gameplay. It demonstrates prompt-based agentic behaviors, vibe coding with Gemma 4, and multiple model-serving deployment options including local inference and cloud APIs.

videos.title: If you don’t run Pi locally you’re falling behind…
videos.summary: David Ondrej argues that running Pi locally is essential to avoid falling behind in AI development. The video promotes building coding agents with Pi and encourages developers to learn AI coding practices.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Just Changed Website Design Forever
videos.summary: Jack Roberts compares Claude Fable 5 against Opus 4.8 for website design generation across three difficulty levels. He argues Fable 5 excels at extracting design patterns and building from visual blueprints, with success hinging on token efficiency and prompt strategy.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is smarter💡
videos.summary: IBM Technology's Chris Hay discusses improvements in Claude Fable 5, highlighting better planning capabilities, deeper code analysis, and improved speed. The segment appears in the Mixture of Experts episode series.

videos.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
videos.summary: Claude FM is a curated music service created by musicians for thinking and building. It provides background music optimized for focused work and creative tasks.

videos.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
videos.summary: Claude FM is a curated music playlist designed to accompany thinking and building activities. It is made and curated by musicians and presented as a tool for productivity and focus.

videos.title: How to Use Higgsfield AI Like a PRO – AI Marketing Workflow
videos.summary: Higgsfield Supercomputer is an AI super-agent that generates multi-format marketing content from a single prompt, including TV ads, podcast clips, vertical reels, and character-driven shorts. The tool maintains character and style consistency across different content formats and workflows, positioning itself as an automated replacement for traditional creative production pipelines.

videos.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL
videos.summary: Google Cloud demonstrates using Gemini to convert SQL Server T-SQL code to PostgreSQL, including data type mappings like IDENTITY and BIT. The AI tutor explains the conversion process to help developers build PostgreSQL expertise during cloud migration.

videos.title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
videos.summary: CopilotKit is a framework enabling developers to build agent-native applications with generative UI that renders real React or Next.js components. It allows AI agents to share state with frontend apps and support human-in-the-loop approval workflows, moving beyond bolted-on chatbot windows in SaaS applications.

videos.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery covers techniques for enabling AI agents to retain and recall information across interactions. The content demonstrates practical implementation approaches for agent memory systems.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her startup built an AI performance management platform that consolidates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data. She shares insights on why even innovative companies like Google rely on spreadsheets, the advantages of building a full enterprise platform from day one, and how Pigment won customers by securing trust from respected companies.

videos.title: Don’t miss this Claude update
videos.summary: Anthropic released Dynamic Workflows in Claude Opus 4.8, enabling the model to launch hundreds of subagents in parallel within a single session. The system automatically plans work, divides tasks, and creates verification workflows where subagents check each other's output.

videos.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
videos.summary: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
videos.summary: The Mixture of Experts podcast discusses Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 release, analyzing its tier routing system as a Mythos-class model. The episode also covers Apple's announcement at WWDC 2026 to partner with NVIDIA for cloud-based AI compute instead of on-device processing, and debates whether AI models can effectively detect sarcasm.

videos.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
videos.summary: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a solution for AI workflows, framing it as an advanced or comprehensive approach to workflow management. The video title suggests UltraCode represents a significant capability in the AI workflow space.

videos.title: Claude Code (Fable) V/S Codex (GPT-5.5) : MATHEMATICAL TEST = ONE CLEAR WINNER!
videos.summary: A video comparing Claude Pro, Claude Max, ChatGPT Plus, and ChatGPT Pro subscription plans against their API-equivalent costs. The analysis estimates that a $20 Claude Pro plan provides roughly $400 in API value while a $200 ChatGPT Pro plan offers up to $14,000 in equivalent usage, concluding that subscriptions offer better value for individual users than APIs do for production workflows.

videos.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
videos.summary: Keith AI's 57-minute video demonstrates advanced use of Claude Code as a coding system rather than a chat interface. The video covers Desktop, CLI, and web modes, permissions, connectors, slash commands, plugins, routines, sub-agents, and dynamic workflows for building apps and automating development workflows.

videos.title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
videos.summary: Anthropic held an opening keynote at Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo, Japan. The event featured updates from Anthropic's engineering and product leaders.

videos.title: See how Gemma  can explore, plan, and scale!
videos.summary: Google presents Gemma alongside ADK, BigQuery MCP, and Cloud Run to demonstrate optimization of business operations through large-scale data exploration. The demonstration showcases the capability to explore, plan, and scale across a vast dataset.

videos.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 is facing backlash for refusing harmless prompts, triggering false alarms, and limiting AI-related work through opaque safety filters and guardrails. Users question whether they receive the full model or a downgraded version shaped behind the scenes. The controversy centers on whether closed AI systems with heavy safety controls can be trusted for their advertised capabilities.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Yossi Matias on the golden age of research
videos.summary: Yossi Matias, VP and Head of Google Research, discusses the "magic cycle" of research at Google, tracing breakthroughs through real-world impact in areas like flood forecasting and scientific discovery. The conversation covers AI tools including Co-Scientist and Empirical Research Assistance, along with technical advances in speculative decoding, generative UI, and quantum computing including the Willow chip.

videos.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech presents a tutorial on building AI-powered agents using the Agent Development Kit and Looker, grounded in enterprise data. The video covers the Model Context Protocol, MCP Toolbox for Databases, tools.yaml configuration, API credential setup, and creating functional agents that translate prompts into data requests via a local web interface.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
videos.summary: A YouTube automation system combining Claude Code Fable 5, Higsfield CLI, and Auphonic to create and edit video content at low cost. The pipeline involves reverse-engineering successful channels, writing scripts, recording voice-overs, and using Claude to automatically edit videos together into finished YouTube content.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: Studio is an internal workspace from WorkOS that allows non-technical users to ask questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion in natural language. The tool generates reusable widgets as declarative JavaScript that call data sources directly, enabling deterministic and efficient subsequent queries without engineering overhead.

videos.title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
videos.summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses scaling coding agents with Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge. The conversation covers running agents at scale in the hundreds, enabling agent-to-agent communication and review, and building emergent systems beyond simple parallelization.

videos.title: Meta' Secret Plan To Kill The Smartphone — With Alex Himel
videos.summary: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, discusses how AI glasses could become the next major computing platform, replacing the smartphone. The conversation covers AI assistants for daily tasks, on-device AI, privacy concerns around facial recognition, and Meta's strategy with Ray-Ban glasses as an AI product.

videos.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates how to run two types of AI agents locally: Odysseus (a workspace agent) for task orchestration and OpenCode (a build agent) for code generation. The setup uses Gemma 4 via Ollama for local inference with Venice API as fallback, deployed in Docker for privacy-focused local execution.

videos.title: 21 Hermes Lessons to Get You Started
videos.summary: Jack Roberts presents 21 lessons or use cases for Hermes, a language model. The content is educational guidance on getting started with the model.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard that provides agents with structured tools and named actions instead of requiring DOM interaction. It offers both a declarative API using HTML attributes and an imperative JavaScript API for registering custom tools. The standard is in early preview on Chrome 146 with eval CLI and inspector extension support.

videos.title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
videos.summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard for enabling AI applications to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a client-server architecture. It uses JSON-RPC for communication and supports both local and remote servers via STDIO or HTTP transports, making it essential for building multi-agent AI systems.

videos.title: Your Attention Is the Bottleneck, Not Your Agents — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser argues that human attention, not tool speed, is the constraint limiting AI agent effectiveness in software development. He proposes a four-layer stack including signal agents to reduce context switching, verification gates for safety, weekly self-improvement loops over conversation history, and holistic well-being monitoring via wearables.

videos.title: Gemini Omni's Best Feature
videos.summary: Google's Gemini Omni can add objects to existing video footage by accepting text, image, or audio descriptions and automatically handling lighting and shading. It enables real-time post-production-style object insertion into existing clips.

videos.title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
videos.summary: Groq is a hardware chip specifically designed for LLM inference. Jonathan Ross, the founder, discusses Groq's LPU architecture, its comparison to GPUs via Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and a strategic agreement with NVIDIA. The interview covers technical aspects like static scheduling and mixture-of-experts models.

videos.title: AI That’s Too Dangerous For You? What we learned from S.A.T.A.N
videos.summary: IBM Technology explains how AI detects zero-day vulnerabilities faster than traditional methods and discusses the implications for cybersecurity risk and defense strategies. The video argues that AI-driven vulnerability scanning and DevSecOps practices could enhance system security.

videos.title: The AI Skill I use to prevent refactors
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery discusses an AI-based skill or technique for preventing code refactors. The content suggests practical application of AI in software development workflows to improve code quality and maintainability.

videos.title: THE $1 CODER: This CODER COSTS $1 AND GIVES YOU $50 WORTH USAGE!
videos.summary: Command Code is a coding agent harness optimized for open-source models including DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, Qwen, and MiniMax. It improves agent performance through routing, caching, context handling, and tool-call repair. The Go plan costs one dollar per month and includes credits for testing open-source models on real coding tasks.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 in three sizes: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture with Latent MoE and MTP techniques for improved performance over standard open models.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is here!
videos.summary: YouTube video reviewing Claude Fable 5 capabilities including reasoning, ray tracing, 3D reconstruction, music composition, and chemistry. Compares performance against GPT 5.5 and discusses model specifications and performance benchmarks.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that simplifies building and deploying AI agents to production. It provides agent identity management, sandboxed code execution, audit trails, and integration with AI coding assistants like Claude. The tool enables rapid agent development and deployment workflows.

videos.title: I One-Shotted An Entire App With Claude Fable 5 (Full Breakdown)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates building a video editing app in one hour by feeding Claude Fable 5 only screenshots of a SaaS landing page via Claude Code. The model successfully generated functional code including filler removal, clip detection, and subtitle export, though Fable 5 consumes API limits 2x faster than Opus and will be discontinued on June 22.

videos.title: 3 rules to keeping your code healthy!
videos.summary: Jasmyne Roberts presents three rules for maintaining healthy code, with emphasis that good identifier naming depends on context rather than length. The guidance is framed as best practices for software development quality.

videos.title: Build a calendar app in AI Studio in 6 minutes
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial demonstrating how to build a no-code calendar application using Google AI Studio and Gemini. The application extracts travel itineraries and converts them into calendar invitations using advanced reasoning capabilities.

videos.title: Don't use Fable 5 in Claude… do this instead
videos.summary: David Ondrej advises against using Fable 5 in Claude and recommends an alternative method. The video discusses Fable 5 as a tool for coding with AI.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel
videos.summary: A 4B model finetuned with RL on tool discipline (first calling get_table_name, inspecting schema, and self-correcting on errors) outperformed a 235B model on database query tasks. The approach transfers from single-table training to multi-table problems and costs under $500, demonstrating that behavioral discipline matters more than scale for this class of structured-query tasks.

videos.title: The Problem Solvers | Michael Truell at Cursor
videos.summary: Cursor is an AI-native coding platform that helps engineers and builders ship code through agents. Founded by Michael Truell, the company has grown from 15 people to 700 and serves over 60% of Fortune 500 companies. Cursor has partnered closely with Anthropic throughout its development.

videos.title: Stop applying AI to the old ways of working!
videos.summary: Andrew Macvean argues that coding was not the primary bottleneck in software development and advocates for rethinking entire workflows and processes around AI capabilities rather than retrofitting AI into existing traditional practices. The emphasis is on evolving development lifecycle thinking for AI rather than applying AI to unchanged legacy approaches.

videos.title: New AI attack reality💥
videos.summary: University of Toronto created an AI worm demonstrating a new cybersecurity threat. The commentary argues that AI systems cannot be stopped and advocates for adapting defenses rather than prevention.

videos.title: PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
videos.summary: PAI (Personal AI Infrastructure) is a Life OS built on Claude Code that provides persistent memory, custom skills, structured workflows, and a seven-phase algorithm to help developers maintain AI context across sessions. It addresses the problem of AI agents forgetting project architecture, coding standards, and past decisions when starting new sessions.

videos.title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves fourth place on LM Arena leaderboard while running on a single GPU, outperforming competitors that require four or five GPUs. The model can run on Pixel phones, be deployed on two GPUs for a hospital, or run multi-agent workloads on Mac. The shift from custom licensing to Apache 2.0 enables sovereign deployment without lengthy procurement review.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Design – Full AI Design Workflow 2026
videos.summary: A tutorial demonstrating Claude Design as a design automation tool that replaces Figma for common workflows. The video covers a four-step framework for building design systems and generating branded landing pages, positioning Claude Design as a junior designer capable of producing production-ready assets from written specifications.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 (TESTED): UHM... It's actually not worth it..
videos.summary: AICodeKing reviews Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, noting strong benchmark performance on coding and specialized domains but concluding it does not represent a major practical improvement over existing models like Opus or GPT-4o for typical workflows. The reviewer cites cost concerns, safety constraints on agentic tasks, and marginal utility gains.

videos.title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
videos.summary: PostHog is building an autonomous system that converts product signals (rage clicks, error spikes, customer messages) into pull requests via a background agent that groups related issues, analyzes the codebase, and opens fixes automatically. The system uses LLM-generated query embeddings for semantic clustering and prioritizes high-specificity signals to improve PR quality.

videos.title: AI agents explained: Build your first agent in 8 minutes
videos.summary: Google Cloud's tutorial demonstrates how to build autonomous multi-agent systems using the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK). The content covers the ReAct Framework and three main agent patterns (sequential, reactive, planning), then walks through building a self-correcting Blog Writing Agent in Python that combines Planner and Writer agents with validation and retry logic.

videos.title: The One Mistake Everyone Makes with AI
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery published a video discussing a widespread mistake that people make when working with AI. The video is framed as educational content for developers integrating AI into applications.

videos.title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
videos.summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast explores whether AI systems are susceptible to social engineering attacks, covering prompt injection vulnerabilities, self-replicating AI worms, and the emerging risk of nonhuman identity compromises in enterprise security. Discusses whether AI agents are more gullible than humans and examines the operational impact of inadequate credential auditing for nonhuman identities.

videos.title: Well, that’s a hot take on light mode for sure!
videos.summary: Google for Developers discusses trade-offs of light mode interfaces, noting that while light mode may increase alertness, there are other costs to consider. The segment features speakers Bri Davis and Justine Lai.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Dropped Fable 5 And It’s Terrifying
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, its first publicly available Mythos-class AI model, which demonstrates strong capabilities in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, analytics, and scientific research. The release includes built-in safety systems that deliberately limit functionality in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and chemistry.

videos.title: $10,000 AI Website in 28mins
videos.summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates building a website comparable to a $10,000 professional project in just 28 minutes using AI tools. The video showcases rapid AI-assisted web development and the efficiency gains from automation.

videos.title: From prompt to a fully working real-time voice app in seconds. 🎙️✨
videos.summary: Google AI Studio enables rapid development and deployment of real-time voice AI applications from a single voice prompt. The platform transforms the development lifecycle toward dynamic, agentic workflows without requiring traditional static code.

videos.title: Apple’s New Siri AI Is Now Gemini
videos.summary: Apple rebuilt Siri at WWDC 2026 with personal context, screen awareness, Visual Intelligence, and deeper cross-device integration powered by Google Gemini. The launch faced investor skepticism, stock decline, and regulatory delays in the EU and China.

videos.title: Claude Mythos is FINALLY here (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos class model, which achieves top performance on benchmarks including SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code. The model supports long-context tasks, vision capabilities, and is available with updated pricing and data retention policies.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 beats Pokémon FireRed only using vision
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 successfully completed Pokémon FireRed using only raw game screenshots and vision capabilities, without requiring helper harnesses or navigation aids that earlier Claude models needed. The demonstration shows the model's improved ability to process visual input and make sequential decisions in a complex game environment.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers addresses job market resilience and career advancement in an AI-driven landscape. The discussion covers networking strategies, personal branding, and dispels fears of junior developer replacement by drawing parallels to historical job evolution in transportation.

videos.title: 21 Hermes concepts to get you started
videos.summary: Jack Roberts presents 21 foundational concepts related to Hermes, a model or framework. The video serves as an introductory guide for learners new to the system.

videos.title: Multiple claude agent in the same window?
videos.summary: Claude Opus 4.8 introduces Dynamic Workflows, which enables launching hundreds of subagents concurrently within a single session. The system automatically plans work distribution, assigns parallel tasks, and performs verification through dedicated quality assurance agents.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

videos.title: Claude just dropped UltraCode... Unlock SuperPowers
videos.summary: Claude released UltraCode, a feature that enables code-based orchestration of up to 10 parallel agents without a master LLM. The video tutorial covers implementation patterns including judge panels, adversarial verification, deterministic fan-out, and integration with OpenRouter for dynamic model selection.

videos.title: Build an app from your phone in AI Studio
videos.summary: Google AI Studio enables users to build custom applications directly from a mobile browser using voice commands and API integrations. The tutorial demonstrates creating an image-generation app without installing software, with code generated in real time through AI reasoning.

videos.title: Behind the Scenes - Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 1 - Playing
videos.summary: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming offered through edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare. The video is a behind-the-scenes introduction to artificial intelligence taught by Brian Yu, Chapter 1 focusing on playing and game-solving approaches.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a deployment tool that simplifies GPU cloud provisioning by allowing developers to decorate async Python functions with @flash.endpoint for direct cloud deployment from their IDE with hot reload. RunPod uses Flash to enable per-second billing on GPU resources like H100s and auto-scaling serverless workers.

videos.title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
videos.summary: Military historian Sarah Paine presents a two-thousand-year geopolitical framework dividing global powers into continental empires (China, Russia) pursuing security through territorial conquest and maritime powers (Britain, US) pursuing prosperity through trade. The framework explains how the Industrial Revolution altered these dynamics and informs contemporary actions by Putin.

videos.title: Claude Mythos 5 + Fable 5 Are Here And The Numbers Are INSANE
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models with new capabilities. Fable 5 is Mythos 5 with guardrails and is available at no extra cost on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscription plans through June 22, after which usage will require separate credits.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 plays Factorio
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 autonomously plays Factorio, a complex factory-building game that requires strategic planning and resource management. The demonstration highlights the model's ability to reason about multi-step goals and execute long-horizon tasks without human intervention.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 simulates the solar system and predicts a solar eclipse
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 built a solar system simulation by deriving planetary orbital motion from physics first principles. The model used this simulation to predict solar eclipses.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 sets a fluid simulation to Beethoven
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 generated code for a fluid simulation synchronized to the beat of an EDM remix of Beethoven. The model also produced the music itself using code, despite having no prior exposure to audio.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 designs a 3D-printable model in a Claude-built CAD editor
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 is demonstrated designing a 3D-printable model using a browser-based CAD editor. The CAD editor itself, including its built-in AI copilot, was created by Fable 5.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead but rather narrowly defined. The real issue is not retrieval itself but how it is implemented. Modern approaches like Cursor combine vector search, full text search, regex, and filters in iterative agent-driven workflows, treating embeddings as cached compute to optimize for query volume.

videos.title: GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening with cuML and Agent Platform
videos.summary: Google Cloud and NVIDIA demonstrated GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening for molecular prediction tasks at scale, achieving 20x-45x training speedups. The approach uses cuDF and cuML on tabular data to predict ligand binding likelihood without rewriting pandas or scikit-learn code.

videos.title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemini 3 Flash Preview processes audio with speaker labels, timestamps, emotion tags, language detection, and summaries in a single API call. The stack includes speech generation directed by natural language prompts and Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a real-time sound-to-sound multimodal model. Integration with Lyria 3 enables full song generation with lyrics via tool use.

videos.title: Stop Prompting Claude. Use Karpathy's Method Instead.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese presents a three-layer approach attributed to Andrej Karpathy for using Claude to accelerate software development. The method is positioned as an alternative to standard prompting practices and aims to unlock the full potential of Claude and Claude Code for building applications faster.

videos.title: How To Design a Complete Brand Identity With AI (Best AI Logo Maker)
videos.summary: Design.com enables rapid brand identity creation including logo generation, brand kits, business cards, social media templates, and animated logos. The platform uses natural language editing and auto-applies brand guidelines across 500,000+ templates without manual recoloring.

videos.title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
videos.summary: Google introduced Gemini 3.5 Live Translate, a speech-to-speech translation model supporting 70+ languages with real-time multilingual translation and live video dubbing. The model is available to developers via the Gemini API and integrates with AI Studio, Google Translate on iOS and Android, and Google Meet.

videos.title: Kimi Code is good at video reasoning with Kimi K2.6 🎬
videos.summary: Kimi Code is a video reasoning tool built on Kimi K2.6 that accepts reference videos and generates .cube LUT (color lookup table) files based on visual queries about colors, shots, or visual style. Users can drag in video references and request specific visual attributes to generate ready-to-use color grading outputs.

videos.title: How I'd Learn AI From Scratch in 2026 (skip the useless 80%)
videos.summary: Jeff Su presents a prioritized learning strategy for AI in 2026 centered on picking one model to master, building practical context through real projects, and assembling AI systems with tools. The guidance dismisses rote prompting frameworks in favor of deeper model understanding and contextual work.

videos.title: Finally… A NotebookLM Alternative With an API
videos.summary: Open Notebook is a self-hosted, open-source alternative to Google NotebookLM that allows developers to manage documents, code, research, and AI workflows with an API. It supports features like grounded question-answering over custom sources and podcast generation from documents.

videos.title: This is why my AI Agents never guess
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery discusses why their AI agents are designed to avoid guessing or uncertainty in their responses. The video appears to promote a principle of deterministic or reliable AI agent behavior.

videos.title: Building AI Agent Systems and Scaling Challenges in Agentic AI
videos.summary: Sam Anthony discusses scaling challenges in agentic AI systems, including increased cost, latency, and failure risk. The video covers how multi-agent design, coordination, and system architecture impact real-world scalability of AI agents.

videos.title: Code with Claude 2026 | Tokyo
videos.summary: Anthropic held Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo, a conference featuring announcements about Claude models, the Claude Platform, and Claude Code, with demonstrations from partner organizations Canva, Mizuho, and NRI.

videos.title: Frontier Code (GPT-5.6 VS Mythos): This BENCHMARK is ACTUALLY REAL!
videos.summary: Cognition released FrontierCode, a benchmark that measures code mergeability and production quality rather than just test passing. It uses blocker criteria, weighted scores, and maintainer-defined rubrics applied to tasks sourced from 36 open-source repositories, with results showing Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 performance across multiple subsets.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: Robot Duck
videos.summary: Google demonstrates Gemma 4 E2B running on-device on a Raspberry Pi 5 via LiteRT and a Jetson Orin Nano, powering two Open Duck Mini v2 robots with real-time voice and vision capabilities. The system uses Parakeet for speech-to-text, Gemma 4 for inference, and Kokoro for text-to-speech to enable the ducks to listen, look, and speak interactively.

videos.title: New BEST local AI image generator is here!
videos.summary: Ideogram 4 is presented as an open-source local AI image generator with capabilities in text rendering, prompt adherence, bounding box control, and comics/manga generation. The tutorial demonstrates its integration with ComfyUI for advanced workflow control.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: AI Engineer is promoting the World's Fair, a conference scheduled for June 29 to July 2, 2026 in San Francisco. Tickets and schedule information are available at ai.engineer/wf.

videos.title: Graphify + Obsidian + Claude Code = CHEAT CODE
videos.summary: Chase AI demonstrates integrating Graphify for repository deconstruction with Obsidian for knowledge graph organization to improve Claude Code's responses. The workflow combines code analysis with note-taking and AI capabilities to enhance software development and documentation tasks.

videos.title: ChatGPT Is Getting Its Biggest Upgrade Ever
videos.summary: ChatGPT is undergoing a major overhaul to become a unified AI super app integrating coding tools, agents, image processing, third-party apps, and workplace automation. OpenAI is developing Lockdown Mode for data protection and collaborating with Broadcom on a custom 10-gigawatt AI accelerator.

videos.title: Working Like a Lawyer with Claude
videos.summary: Mark Pike from Anthropic and Anna Gressel from Freshfields discuss how lawyers are using Claude in their work. They explore how legal work is changing with AI adoption while emphasizing that final judgment decisions remain with human lawyers.

videos.title: Stop managing infrastructure for AI Agents 🛠️
videos.summary: Google announced managed Agents in the Gemini API that handle infrastructure provisioning automatically. A single API call deploys production-ready autonomous agents running in a Google-hosted sandbox, eliminating the need for developers to manage servers or infrastructure.

videos.title: Intro to multi-agent systems with ADK
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial introducing the Agent Development Kit, a framework for building and testing multi-agent systems. The video covers creating agents, equipping them with custom tools like real-time date retrieval and Google Search, and using built-in debugging interfaces.

videos.title: Claude Code + Graphify = Insane Agentic OS
videos.summary: Graphify builds a knowledge graph representation of code projects, allowing Claude to understand repository structure without re-reading entire codebases. This reduces token costs, latency, and improves accuracy when working with Claude Code for agentic systems.

videos.title: Road to 5 Million Tokens: Breaking Barriers in Long Context Training — Max Ryabinin, Together AI
videos.summary: Max Ryabinin presents a full-stack approach to training LLMs with multi-million token context windows. The method combines fully sharded data parallelism, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, CPU offloading, and chunked sequence training. A novel contribution called Untied Ulysses further optimizes context parallelism by chunking attention heads and reusing buffers, achieving up to 5 million token context lengths at 8B and 32B model scales.

videos.title: Reflecting on a year of Claude Code
videos.summary: Claude Code, an agentic coding tool running in the terminal, reached its one-year general availability milestone. The session features leaders Boris Cherny and Cat Wu discussing adoption across engineering teams and organizations, design choices like auto mode, verification strategies, and the expansion of AI agents beyond traditional engineering roles.

videos.title: AI YouTube Automation: How to Start a YouTube Channel With AI
videos.summary: AI Master presents a complete YouTube video creation workflow from trending research through publishing using AI tools. The video walks through 11 stages including ChatGPT for ideation, Claude for briefs, HeyGen for avatars, Descript for editing, and Opus Clip for repurposing content into shorts. Arthur Winer demonstrates creating a publishable YouTube video in 20 minutes using agent-driven automation across content creation, design, and publishing.

videos.title: Simulating Percolation in Python: How Do Wildfires & Diseases Spread?
videos.summary: This video tutorial demonstrates how to implement percolation simulations in Python to model the spread of wildfires and diseases. Percolation theory is used to understand how phenomena propagate through networks and systems.

videos.title: Did Apple (Finally) Get AI Right At WWDC?, Anthropic’s Worry, Microsoft vs. OpenAI
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss Apple's AI strategy at WWDC, Anthropic's claims about AI self-improvement capabilities, and the emerging competition between Microsoft and OpenAI. The conversation examines whether Apple is finally aligning AI features with actual user capabilities, questions around Anthropic's marketing claims, and tensions between AI safety and commercial pressure in the industry.

videos.title: Inside Google Antigravity 2.0: The complete developer guide | The Agent Factory
videos.summary: Google Antigravity 2.0 is an AI-powered development platform that unbundles the IDE into a standalone agent manager, CLI, SDK, and custom IDE. It enables developers to use voice prompts to orchestrate multiple specialized sub-agents for building, testing, and deploying applications at scale.

videos.title: Hermes Agent is crazy… 180,000+ github stars
videos.summary: David Ondrej reviews Hermes Agent, a project with over 180,000 GitHub stars, and demonstrates how running it with MiniMax's m3 model significantly reduces operational costs. The video highlights the agent's capabilities and practical efficiency gains.

videos.title: How A Beauty Company Built An AI Agent — With Rachel Williamson and Josh Siebert
videos.summary: Ulta Beauty deployed ServiceNow's AI-powered HR platform across its 65,000-person workforce to automate HR processes including hiring and onboarding. The deployment involved decisions about task automation versus human-in-the-loop workflows and changed hiring plans during rollout.

videos.title: Why More Context Makes Your Agent Dumber and What to Do About It — Nupur Sharma, Qodo
videos.summary: Nupur Sharma argues that expanding context windows alone does not solve agent performance degradation, which manifests as the U curve effect where agents ignore middle context. She proposes iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self correction as solutions, and describes an orchestration strategy pairing high-reasoning models for discovery with lighter deterministic models for validation.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that rabid fans and formal customer interviews are poor signals of true product-market fit. He contends that real PMF manifests as customer pull rather than push, and advises founders to embed deeply in customer operations while betting on the future direction of AI models rather than their current state.

videos.title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare
videos.summary: Cloudflare's Dynamic Workers enable safe execution of LLM-generated code in sandboxed isolates with explicit permission grants. The system avoids ambient access and is positioned as a compute primitive for AI agents. Cloudflare is shipping a coding agent harness built entirely on Workers.

videos.title: Can you spot which committed file explains this mismatch?
videos.summary: Google for Developers presents a package management puzzle where local development and CI install the same dependency but end up with different versions despite identical package.json files. The video walkthrough challenges viewers to identify which committed file explains the mismatch by analyzing how the install is being resolved.

videos.title: Headroom: The Netflix Tool That Makes AI Agents 10x Cheaper
videos.summary: Headroom is an open-source tool that compresses inputs to LLMs using content-type-aware compressors and locally trained models, reducing token usage by 60-95%. Built by a Netflix engineer, it keeps compression local and uses breadcrumb hashing for on-demand retrieval of full original content.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model incorporating MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) to replace full attention mechanisms. The model includes tiled KV cache optimizations and I/O improvements that reduce prefill and decode latency by reading cache continuously and efficiently between HBM and SRAM.

videos.title: What Is Agentic Coding? How AI Agents Modernize Code
videos.summary: IBM Technology explains agentic coding as an AI-driven approach where agents help developers modernize legacy systems and accelerate software development workflows. The video argues that developers spend more time understanding code than building it, and that AI tools can reduce risk and improve DevSecOps outcomes.

videos.title: Claude Oceanus V1-P (Mythos?- FULLY TESTED): I TESTED IT & IT'S ACTUALLY CRAZY!
videos.summary: A YouTuber tested a leaked model called Oceanus V1-P (possibly linked to "Mythos") across coding, reasoning, visual, and game tasks using OpenCode benchmarks. The model achieved perfect scores and reportedly outperformed comparable models like Claude Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini 3.5 Flash.

videos.title: I Turned Cheap Cloud Storage Into a 1PB Local Drive (With JuiceFS)
videos.summary: JuiceFS is a POSIX-compliant virtual file system that layers cloud object storage like AWS S3 with a multi-tiered caching engine using local NVMe for performance. The video demonstrates setting up JuiceFS with Redis metadata, configuring local SSD cache, and running AI inference workloads directly against cloud buckets while monitoring performance via Prometheus and Better Stack observability.

videos.title: Start a 1-Person AI Creative Agency with Higgsfield + Claude
videos.summary: Liam Ottley demonstrates how to build a one-person AI creative agency using Higgsfield for image generation, Claude for automation and copywriting, Notion for client databases, and Apify for competitor scraping. The system produces product photography, UGC ads, commercials, and ad copy while automatically tracking performance and competitor data.

videos.title: the most POWERFUL claude code feature in months
videos.summary: Graphify is a non-RAG approach for analyzing large codebases by creating knowledge graphs to store extracted information. It enables more accurate, token-efficient responses without relying on traditional retrieval-augmented generation methods.

videos.title: Did Google Just Fall Behind Again?, WWDC Preview, Anthropic Files To Go Public
videos.summary: M.G. Siegler discusses whether Google is falling behind OpenAI and Anthropic in AI, with focus on coding agents and super-app ambitions. The conversation covers how AI agents may reshape web, email, apps, and browser usage, potentially disadvantaging Google, plus coverage of Apple WWDC, Meta subscription strategy, and Anthropic's IPO plans.

videos.title: Harness Engineering Is AI’s New Gold Rush
videos.summary: Harness engineering represents a systems-level approach to AI that extends beyond prompting by integrating tools, memory, context, permissions, verification, recovery paths, and feedback loops around AI agents. This technique enables the same model to become significantly more reliable and useful over time through architectural improvements rather than prompt modification alone.

videos.title: Big Tech Is Buying Up Open Source... Vite Is Next
videos.summary: Cloudflare acquired VoidZero, the company behind Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, and Oxc. The video examines the broader trend of big tech companies acquiring open source developer tooling and discusses the potential implications for open source ecosystems.

videos.title: LLM Observability, Evaluation, Experimentation Platform — Dat Ngo, Arize
videos.summary: Dat Ngo from Arize discusses LLM observability challenges in nondeterministic systems. Arize Phoenix is an open-source observability platform deployed as a single container, while the enterprise product adds an AI-driven layer called Alex that automatically scans traces, surfaces anomalies, and generates evaluations.

videos.title: Under 5 minutes to a deployed LLM endpoint — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: RunPod is a serverless LLM deployment platform enabling users to deploy LLM endpoints in under five minutes on H100 GPUs. The platform started in 2022 from repurposed crypto mining hardware and has grown to 500,000 developers with $120 million in annual recurring revenue, charging only for active request handling time.

videos.title: From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Rafael Levi demonstrates how Bright Data's Model Context Protocol integrates with agents to automate web scraper generation, maintenance, and repair. The system uses agents to inspect site structures, generate production scrapers, and self-repair pipelines when websites change.

videos.title: Reinventing Entropy | Compression is Intelligence Part 1
videos.summary: 3Blue1Brown's video explores how compression relates to intelligence by studying the compressibility of language through information theory. The content visualizes entropy and draws on Shannon's foundational work on communication and prediction, presenting a perspective that understanding data compression is fundamental to understanding intelligence.

videos.title: Kubernetes vs. OpenShift: Choosing DevOps and CI/CD Workflows
videos.summary: IBM Technology compares Kubernetes and OpenShift as container platforms, examining how each shapes DevOps workflows, CI/CD pipelines, and operational delivery speed. The video explores platform selection decisions affecting team processes and infrastructure choices.

videos.title: Antigravity 3.0 (New Upgrades): Gemini 3.6, Agent Team-Up Option, Workbench, Low Thinking Effort
videos.summary: Google released Antigravity 3.0 with new features including slash teamwork for multi-agent orchestration, a Science Skills bundle for research workflows, improved Gemini 3.5 Flash models, and session syncing across CLI and desktop interfaces.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live Q&A stream on Saturdays at 9 AM CT where viewers can ask questions about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream combines interactive discussion with casual chess gameplay.

videos.title: Full body waifus, AI dreams, realtime AI music, open-source Gemini Omni: AI NEWS
videos.summary: AI Search presents a digest of recent AI tool and model releases including Minimax M3, Ideogram v4, Gemma4, Nemotron 3 Ultra, Cosmos 3, WavTTS, and others. The roundup covers image generation, language models, video models, audio synthesis, and reasoning frameworks.

videos.title: Google's New AI Architecture Changes Everything (Gemma 4 12B)
videos.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemma 4 12B, a multimodal LLM that processes images and audio by reading raw pixels and waveforms directly instead of using separate encoder models. The architecture eliminates intermediate encoder models, enabling faster offline performance on consumer hardware like Apple Silicon.

videos.title: The Top 10 Claude Code Plugins to 10x Your Next Project (June '26)
videos.summary: Chase AI presents ten tools and plugins designed to enhance Claude Code workflow, including Graphify for knowledge graph generation from code bases, grill-me for skill documentation, NotebookLM integration, and n8n MCP for workflow automation. videos.summary: Qdrant has launched a Multi-Vector Search Course available at their course portal. The course covers multi-vector search capabilities, a technique for retrieving information across multiple vector representations simultaneously.

videos.title: Late Interaction Basics | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Late interaction is a search paradigm that preserves token-level representations while enabling pre-computation of documents, contrasted against single-vector embeddings and cross-encoders. The video explains how late interaction models like ColBERT balance retrieval speed with relevance precision for large-scale collections.

videos.title: Use Cases for Multi-Vector Search | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Lesson comparing single-vector and multi-vector search approaches using BGE versus ColBERT on technical support queries. Multi-vector search with token-level verification prevents false positives from keyword-stuffed content by requiring strong matches at the token level rather than just topical overlap.

videos.title: Problems of Multi-Vector Search | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Multi-vector search improves retrieval quality but requires brute-force scanning because HNSW indexing fails with MaxSim due to its asymmetric, query-dependent nature. Storing hundreds of token embeddings per document incurs roughly 167x more memory overhead than single-vector approaches, creating significant computational and storage costs at scale.

videos.title: Multi-Vector Embeddings in Qdrant | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Tutorial on configuring Qdrant for multi-vector search using the MaxSim comparator, indexing documents with FastEmbed local inference, and querying with ColBERT embeddings. Covers collection setup, HNSW disabling for MaxSim compatibility, and on-disk vector storage for memory-constrained environments.

videos.title: How ColPali Models Work | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: ColPali extends late interaction from text to visual documents by using a Vision Language Model (PaliGemma-3B) to divide document images into a 32x32 grid of patches, each producing visual token embeddings. Text queries match directly against these visual patches using MaxSim scoring without requiring OCR.

videos.title: ColPali Family Overview | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: The ColPali family includes compact embedding models (ColSmol, ColFlor, ColModernVBERT) for visual document retrieval with reduced parameters compared to the base ColPali. Multilingual variants from NVIDIA and Nomic AI are available, and ViDoRe V3 serves as the benchmark for evaluating visual retrieval models. Qdrant is a vector search engine in Rust supporting semantic and hybrid search.

videos.title: Visual Interpretability of ColPali | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Qdrant demonstrates visual interpretability for ColPali embeddings, which represent documents as 1,024 patch embeddings mapped to spatial locations. The system computes per-token similarity heatmaps overlaid on documents to show which regions match query terms, enabling interpretable multi-vector search and relevance debugging.

videos.title: Multi-Stage Retrieval with Universal Query API | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Qdrant's prefetch plus rerank pattern uses fast initial retrieval (single-vector or BM25) to narrow candidates down from millions to hundreds, then applies ColBERT reranking to refine results. The technique emphasizes oversampling in stage one to ensure sufficient candidates for effective reranking.

videos.summary: Video demonstrates a technique for rapidly building high-end animated websites using Claude Code for development, Kling video generation, and Nano Banana for image synthesis. The creator combines Leon Lin's taste skill with generative tools to produce custom websites reportedly worth $15K in a streamlined workflow.

videos.title: Stop Fixing Your Claude Skills. Autoresearch Does It For You
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to automatically improve Claude Code skills by combining Karpathy's autoresearch principles with evals. The approach provides a step-by-step walkthrough for practitioners to optimize their own LLM-based skills through automated evaluation and refinement.

videos.title: How to Make Your AI Agents Work Better (With Context Hygiene)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs presents context hygiene as a technique for maintaining AI agent performance across multiple messages and conversations. The approach involves three layers, Skills, Harnesses, and Hygiene, along with practices like lazy loading and multi-agent orchestration to keep context windows clean and avoid re-explaining requirements.

videos.title: NotebookLM is now bigger than Gemini
videos.summary: NotebookLM now has higher search interest than Google Gemini. Its core strength is the ability to ingest multi-format documents and provide grounded, accurate answers without hallucinations. It is recommended when accuracy matters and source documents in various formats need to be consulted.

videos.title: 10 years of AlphaGo: The turning point for AI | Thore Graepel & Pushmeet Kohli
videos.summary: A Google DeepMind retrospective marking 10 years since AlphaGo defeated Lee Sedol at Go in March 2016. The video presents AlphaGo as a turning point that catalyzed the modern AI revolution, enabling subsequent breakthroughs in algorithmic discovery and scientific challenges like protein folding.
